2604.16084.title: Unveiling Stochasticity: Universal Multi-modal Probabilistic Modeling for Traffic Forecasting
2604.16084.paper_summary: Traffic forecasting is a challenging spatio-temporal modeling task and a critical component of urban transportation management. Current studies mainly focus on deterministic predictions, with limited considerations on the uncertainty and stochasticity in traffic dynamics. Therefore, this paper proposes an elegant yet universal approach that transforms existing models into probabilistic predictors by replacing only the final output layer with a novel Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) layer. The modified model requires no changes to the training pipeline and can be trained using only the Negative Log-Likelihood (NLL) loss, without any auxiliary or regularization terms. Experiments on multiple traffic datasets show that our approach generalizes from classic to modern model architectures while preserving deterministic performance. Furthermore, we propose a systematic evaluation procedure based on cumulative distributions and confidence intervals, and demonstrate that our approach is considerably more accurate and informative than unimodal or deterministic baselines. Finally, a more detailed study on a real-world dense urban traffic network is presented to examine the impact of data quality on uncertainty quantification and to show the robustness of our approach under imperfect data conditions. Code available at https://github.com/Weijiang-Xiong/OpenSkyTraffic
2604.16084.paper_summary_zh: 交通預測是一項具有挑戰性的時空建模任務，也是城市交通管理的重要組成部分。當前的研究主要集中在確定性預測上，對交通動態中的不確定性和隨機性考量有限。因此，本文提出了一種優雅而通用的方法，通過僅用一個新穎的高斯混合模型（GMM）層替換現有模型的最終輸出層，將其轉變為概率預測器。修改後的模型不需要對訓練流程進行任何更改，並且可以僅使用負對數似然（NLL）損失進行訓練，而無需任何輔助或正則化項。在多個交通數據集上的實驗顯示，我們的方法能夠從經典模型架構推廣到現代模型架構，同時保持確定性性能。此外，我們提出了一種基於累積分佈和置信區間的系統評估程序，並證明我們的方法在準確性和信息量上顯著優於單峰或確定性基準。最後，我們對一個真實的密集城市交通網絡進行了更詳細的研究，以檢查數據質量對不確定性量化的影響，並展示我們的方法在不完美數據條件下的穩健性。代碼可在 https://github.com/Weijiang-Xiong/OpenSkyTraffic 獲得。
2604.16084.author: Weijiang Xiong et.al.
2604.16084.authors: Weijiang Xiong, Robert Fonod, Nikolas Geroliminis
